data.title: Netflix hit with refund order over illegal subscription price hikes in Italy
data.description:
A court in Rome ruled that Netflix (NFLX) had unlawfully increased subscription prices for Italian users between 2017 and 2024, ordering refunds and pricing rollbacks.

The Court of Rome’s Sixteenth Civil Section, on April 3, said Netflix’s (NFLX) price-change clauses were “vexatious and null,” impacting millions of subscribers. The ruling reportedly meant long-term users could receive significant refunds.

Lawyers Paolo Fiorio and Riccardo Pinna, representing consumers in the case, stated that unlawful increases totaled about €8 (~$9.22) per month for Premium plans and €4 (~$4.61) for Standard plans, reported Reuters.

As a result of the court's findings, a Premium subscriber since 2017 may receive around €500 (~$576), while a Standard subscriber could get about €250 (~$288) in refunds.

According to Italy's communication authority, the scale of impact is large, as Netflix (NFLX) had over 8M users in 2024 and 5.4M subscribers in 2025 in Italy.

In addition to these refund instructions, the court also ordered the streaming platform Italia to publish the ruling on its website and in leading newspapers, informing users of their right to refunds.

It has 90 days to comply with the order or face daily penalties of €700 (~$806) per day. Consumer leader Alessandro Mostaccio also warned of a potential class action if refunds are delayed.

The company plans to appeal the decision, while similar complaints have emerged in Poland and Spain over recent price hikes.

data.title: Newport Gold finalizes merger with NFI Empire
data.description:
• Newport Gold (NWPG) announced on Friday it has entered the final stages of its merger with a trio of Pennsylvania Limited Liability Companies known as "NFI Empire" by entering into a formal share exchange agreement. 
  • The company said that the structure enables Newport Gold to pursue growth initiatives without the dilution, complexity, or historical baggage often associated with reverse mergers.
  • Anthony McCabe, CEO of Newport Gold, said, "Following a comprehensive review of our strategic alternatives, it became clear that a merger with NFI delivers the strongest value proposition for our shareholders. NFI is already generating eight-figure revenues with positive profitability, underscoring the strength of its operating model. Combined with its exceptional leadership and clear roadmap for accelerated growth, we are confident this transaction positions the company for long-term success and enhanced shareholder value."
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Atmus Filtration to Join S&P SmallCap 600, Replacing Air Lease
data.description:
• Atmus Filtration Technologies (NYSE: ATMU) said on Friday that it will replace Air Lease in the S&P SmallCap 600, effective before the open of trading on April 9, S&P Global.

  • Air Lease is being acquired by a consortium led by Sumitomo, with the transaction expected to close soon, subject to final conditions.

  • Both companies are classified under the Industrials sector.

data.title: Ridgetech announces 1-for-150 reverse share split
data.description:
• Ridgetech (RDGT) announced on Friday that it expects to implement a 1-for-150 reverse share split on its ordinary shares, effective Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time, with trading to begin on a split‑adjusted basis at the market open on the next trading day.
  • The new CUSIP number for the ordinary shares following the reverse share split is G2124G 203.
  • As of April 2, 2026, the company had 134.83M ordinary shares outstanding; following the reverse share split, the company expects to have approximately 898,906 ordinary shares outstanding. 
  • Upon the effectiveness of the reverse share split, every 150 shares of the company's issued and outstanding ordinary shares will automatically be converted into one issued and outstanding ordinary share, par value $0.15 per share. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Top mid-cap energy stocks ranked by their forward dividend yields
data.description:
As market grapples with volatility, below is a list of the top 10 mid-cap energy stocks ranked by their forward dividend yields. 

The list is topped by Mach Natural Resources LP (MNR), with a dividend yield of 15.08%. Delek Logistics Partners, LP (DKL) and Alliance Resource Partners, L.P. (ARLP) are next, with Black Stone Minerals, L.P. (BSM) and Hess Midstream LP (HESM) rounding out the rest of the top five.

The list includes a variety of industries within the energy sector, including Oil and Gas Storage and Transportation, Oil and Gas Exploration and Production, Coal and Consumable Fuels, and Oil and Gas Equipment and Services. Other notable entries include USA Compression Partners, LP (USAC), Kinetik Holdings Inc. (KNTK), and Plains GP Holdings, L.P. (PAGP).

Here is the list:

  1. Mach Natural Resources LP (MNR), Dividend yield: 15.08%

  2. Delek Logistics Partners, LP Common Units (DKL), Dividend yield: 8.95%

  3. Alliance Resource Partners, L.P. Common Units (ARLP), Dividend yield: 8.52%

  4. Black Stone Minerals, L.P. Common Units (BSM), Dividend yield: 8.19%

  5. Hess Midstream LP (HESM), Dividend yield: 7.88%

  6. USA Compression Partners, LP Common Units (USAC), Dividend yield: 7.60%

  7. Kinetik Holdings Inc. (KNTK), Dividend yield: 6.94%

  8. Plains GP Holdings, L.P. Class A Shares (PAGP), Dividend yield: 6.94%

  9. Northern Oil and Gas, Inc. (NOG), Dividend yield: 6.36%

  10. International Seaways, Inc. (INSW), Dividend yield: 5.81%

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: Block sees lowest interest from short sellers in March among crypto firms with over $2B market cap
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices continued to hover around the $60k to $70k level in March. The already volatile cryptocurrency was further pressured amid the U.S.-Iran conflict. While macroeconomic and geopolitical headwinds persist, diminishing optimism around U.S. crypto legislation has also eroded one of the market’s earlier tailwinds.

Among crypto stocks with a market cap of more than $2B, application software firms witnessed the highest short interest in the previous month. CleanSpark (CLSK) continued to lead the list with short interest rising to 33.08%, followed by MARA Holdings (MARA), which saw SI rise to 30.08% in the month.

Here are the five most shorted crypto stocks with market capitalizations of above $2 billion (as a % of shares outstanding)

CleanSpark (CLSK); Short Interest: 33.08%

MARA Holdings (MARA); Short Interest: 30.08%

Bitdeer Technologies Group (BTDR); Short Interest: 24.27%

TeraWulf (WULF); Short Interest: 22.77%

IREN Limited (IREN); Short Interest: 15.54%

Here are the five least shorted crypto stocks with market capitalizations of above $2 billion (as a % of shares outstanding)

Block (XYZ); Short Interest: 4.32%

Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR); Short Interest: 4.61%

Bullish (BLSH); Short Interest: 6.40%

Hycroft Mining Holding (HYMC); Short Interest: 8.28%

Circle Internet Group (CRCL); Short Interest: 9.25%

data.title: DeFi Development records highest short interest in March, while Bitgo lags among firms with up to $2B market cap
data.description:
Among crypto companies with a market cap of up to $2B, DeFi Development (DFDV) continued to see the highest interest from short sellers, with an SI of 21.20%. Strive Asset Management was second on the list with an SI of 20.97%.

Bitgo (BTGO) saw the lowest interest from short sellers in this segment with an SI of 1.99%. Nano Labs (NA) and Nakamoto (NAKA) followed with SI of 2.25% and 2.58%, respectively.

Here are the five most shorted crypto stocks with market capitalizations of up to $2 billion (as a % of shares outstanding)

data.title: Versant acquires AI-driven financial insights platform StockStory
data.description:
Versant Media Group (VSNT), the networks-focused entity that spun out of Comcast (CMCSA) and the parent company of CNBC and MS NOW, said it has acquired AI-powered StockStory to support growth for CNBC’s digital platform.

Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.

StockStory delivers financial analysis, market insights, and stock recommendations. Its technology focuses on analysis of public companies, combining data, machine learning, AI, and editorial frameworks to generate investment insights. 

“StockStory’s AI-driven technology will strengthen CNBC’s ability to provide data-driven insights with faster, more actionable analysis to help investors make informed investment decisions in real time,” the company said.

As part of the transaction, the company said founder and CEO of StockStory Adam Hejl will join Versant and report to Deep Bagchee, Chief Product and Technology Officer for News.

Versant has been aggressive in its pursuit of deals since it debuted on the Nasdaq earlier this year. So far, it has bought Free TV Networks, a provider of over-the-air digital broadcasts, and INDY Cinema Group, a technology platform for movie theaters.

According to a report from The New York Times in March, Versant is also weighing buying Vox Media's podcast network.

data.title: DeepSeek to use Huawei chips for new V4 model: report
data.description:
China's DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) will use chips designed by Huawei Technologies to run its new model V4, The Information reported, citing five people with direct ​knowledge of ​the ⁠purchase.

Ahead of V4’s launch, Chinese tech giants including Alibaba Group, ByteDance, and Tencent Holdings have reportedly placed bulk orders for Huawei’s upcoming chips, totaling hundreds of thousands of units.

The next-generation model is likely to be launched in the coming weeks. DeepSeek is also developing two additional V4 variants, each tailored for different capabilities and designed to run on Chinese-made chips, the report said.

The Hangzhou-based AI startup has spent the past few months working directly with Huawei and another Chinese chip designer, Cambricon Technologies, to rewrite and test the model’s underlying code, as per the report that cited two people close to the company.

data.title: Meta Platforms and Google face jury setbacks; Section 230 under pressure
data.description:
A major legal shift may be underway for Big Tech companies, as protections under Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act come under pressure.

Last week, juries in Los Angeles and New Mexico reportedly delivered key verdicts against Meta Platforms (META) and Google (GOOGL).

In Los Angeles, jurors reportedly found META and YouTube negligent in a personal injury case, awarding ~$6M to a young woman who began using the apps as a child.

Meanwhile, in New Mexico, the jury ordered Meta Platforms (META) to pay $375M for failing to protect minors from child predators. Attorney General Raúl Torrez said he will seek further penalties and push for app changes to improve safety, CNBC reported.

Plaintiffs argued that design features like autoplay, recommendation algorithms, and notifications in Instagram and YouTube acted like “digital casinos,” driving addictive use and mental harm of minors.

Alongside this, new lawsuits are also emerging around AI tools, including claims that Google's (GOOGL) systems exposed sensitive personal data. Lawmakers are also raising pressures, as Senator Brian Schatz said tech firms have long relied on Section 230 as a shield to avoid action because fixing harms could hurt profits.

Both the companies said they plan to appeal. Legal expert David Greene called the verdicts “very preliminary decisions,” adding that simply labeling something as a design feature does not remove potential legal protections.
